title: Double chocolate muffins
subtitle: Bakery-style muffins with cocoa batter and chocolate chips
excerpt: Bakery-style chocolate muffins with a domed, cracked top and a dense, fudgy crumb. Cocoa in the batter, chocolate chips throughout. Mixed in one bowl, ready in 30 minutes. The oil and buttermilk are what give them the moist, tender crumb that holds for several days.
sourceNotes: The large, domed American-style muffin with a cracked top became the café standard across the English-speaking world from the 1980s onwards. The double chocolate variant (cocoa batter plus chocolate chips) is one of the most established café muffin types. The recipe here synthesises the oil-and-buttermilk enriched muffin method documented in American domestic baking literature from the second half of the twentieth century, adapted for the British kitchen. Buttermilk is the key ingredient for the open, tender crumb.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze individually once cool. Thaw at room temperature for 2 hours.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Best the day they are baked, good for two days. Warm briefly in a microwave or low oven the next day.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: conventional oven. Fan at 180°C. A high starting temperature sets the dome; reduce to 180°C (fan 160°C) after the first 5 minutes if you find the tops browning before the centres are set.

glossaryTerms.definition: The characteristic high, cracked top of a bakery muffin, produced by a high oven temperature and a thick, slightly overfilled batter. The outside sets quickly before the interior has expanded, forcing the rise through the softer centre. Two conditions produce a good dome: the batter must be thick (not pourable), and the tins must be filled almost to the top. Underfilled muffins produce a flat top; a thin batter produces a flat top regardless of fill level.

content.text: is the result of a thick batter and a hot oven. Fill the cases higher than feels comfortable (almost to the top) and do not open the oven door in the first 10 minutes. The chocolate chips can go into the batter or scattered on top for a different look; a mixture of both gives chips throughout and a visibly studded top.

attrs.prepNote: dark or milk, or a mix

content.text: Heat the oven to 200°C conventional. Line a 12-hole muffin tin with paper cases or grease each well. Sift the flour, cocoa, sugar, baking powder, bicarbonate of soda, and salt into a large bowl. In a jug, whisk together the eggs, buttermilk, oil, and vanilla. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry and fold together with a spatula using 12 to 15 strokes until just combined. The batter will be thick and lumpy; do not beat it smooth. Fold in three-quarters of the chocolate chips.

Divide between the cases, filling each one almost to the top. Scatter the remaining chocolate chips over the surface. Bake for 20 to 22 minutes until the tops are cracked and set, the centres do not wobble when the tin is nudged, and a skewer through the centre comes out with moist (not wet) crumbs. Cool in the tin for 5 minutes, then transfer to a rack.
